title: Build a Media Strategy
description: Create a comprehensive media strategy for a political figure or cause — covering earned media, social media, crisis preparation, and a 12-week content calendar.
whyItMatters: In modern politics, if you don't have a media strategy, you don't have a strategy. Media coverage shapes how voters perceive candidates and issues. A deliberate media strategy ensures you are driving the narrative rather than reacting to it.

steps.title: Audit the current landscape
steps.description: Map the media landscape for your electorate or issue: which outlets cover it, who are the key journalists, what is the current narrative?
steps.tips: Include traditional media, digital outlets, local publications, and social media influencers.

steps.title: Define your media objectives
steps.description: Set three specific goals: e.g., 'Establish candidate as the leading voice on housing in 3 months.'
steps.tips: Goals should be measurable. 'Get more media coverage' is not a goal.

steps.title: Develop key messages
steps.description: Write three key messages and test them: can you say each in under 15 seconds? Do they work on radio, TV, print, and social?
steps.tips: The best messages pass the 'dinner table test' — can a regular person repeat it to their family?

steps.title: Build the content calendar
steps.description: Create a 12-week content calendar: one earned media opportunity per week, three social media posts per week, one longer-form piece per month.
steps.tips: Align content with the parliamentary calendar, local events, and seasonal news cycles.

steps.title: Prepare crisis protocols
steps.description: Write crisis response templates for three likely scenarios: a personal attack, a policy failure, and an unexpected external event.
steps.tips: The best crisis preparation is having the templates ready so you can respond in hours, not days.

steps.title: Measure and iterate
steps.description: Define how you will measure success: media mentions, social media engagement, message penetration in focus groups.
steps.tips: Review weekly and adjust. A strategy that doesn't evolve is a strategy that fails.

deliverables: A media contact list for the electorate

rubric.description: Do the media objectives, messages, and content calendar align with the overall political strategy?
Are the key messages clear, memorable, and deliverable in under 15 seconds?
Is the content calendar achievable with available resources and aligned with the news cycle?
Are the crisis templates specific, actionable, and realistic?
Is there a clear plan for measuring media strategy effectiveness?
